notes:
I run Arch Linux.  Quench launched flawlessly when I set the environment variable, PROTON_USE_WINED3D11.  Specifically, my cmdline was:  "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 steam-runtime" (steam-runtime is already in my executable path).

One minor issue was that my mainboard CPUs ran hot (they got up to about 98 degrees Celcius before I exited the game).  But also, I was running Steam in a configuration that didn't fully utilize my NVIDIA GPU.  That is, my laptop has an onboard Intel GPU, as well as NVIDIA secondary GPU.  Anyone who has tried use a box like that for Linux gaming might be familiar with nvidia-xrun.  Well... When I launched Quench, I wasn't running nvidia-xrun.

So I expect that with nvidia-xrun, I can offload some of the work the mainboard CPU was doing onto the NVIDIA.  (I probably should've tried that before writing this report :D)

But anyway, Quench runs!
title: Quench
